steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage presents community science as effective for two reasons: it builds youth confidence in science, and it greatly expands the amount of data researchers can collect. The signal words 'also' and 'very effective because' mark both as central claims.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A community science approach can increase youth confidence in learning science and generate large amounts of data, benefiting both communities and researchers.: Names both benefits the passage explicitly states — building youth confidence in science and greatly expanding the data researchers can collect. Matches the passage's two central claims. ✓
  • Choice B — A community science approach can benefit communities by increasing youth confidence in learning science, but it's less likely to help researchers obtain accurate data.: Claims community science is effective only because it engages young people, omitting the data-collection benefit the passage also presents as a reason for its effectiveness. ✗
  • Choice C — Clara Benton and colleagues used a community science approach in their butterfly research and were surprised at the effect this had on youth confidence in learning science.: Focuses on a single detail (e.g., one specific community science project or example) rather than summarizing the overall argument for why community science is effective. ✗
  • Choice D — A community science approach allows researchers to collect significantly more data even though it's unlikely to have much of an effect on youth confidence in learning science.: Claims community science is effective only because it expands data collection, omitting the youth-confidence benefit the passage also explicitly highlights. ✗
That matches Choice A — A community science approach can increase youth confidence in learning science and generate large amounts of data, benefiting both communities and researchers..
